summary: Kenneth demonstrates a systematic covered-call selling approach targeting $180/week premium on individual stocks (HIMS, OLO, JOBY, Oscar Health). He achieved $1,192 in premiums on $25,930 deployed capital (4.6% weekly return) while emphasizing the strategy's income focus, position sizing discipline, and risks around earnings events and assignment.
takeaways: Target $180-200 per week in premium per covered-call position as attainable goal (vs. unsustainable claims of thousands/week)
Sell the highest premium strike price even if slightly out-of-money (e.g., sold $49.50 strike on HIMS bought at $49.57 for 7-cent loss per share acceptable for premium maximization)
Scale into new positions smaller initially (e.g., $2,100 on new Oscar Health vs. $15,000 on established HIMS) until familiar with stock behavior
Use 1-2 week expirations (weekly or monthly) to maintain flexibility and compound premium income
Avoid wheel strategy on volatile stocks showing pullback risk (e.g., JOBY up to 52-week highs, avoided CSP to prevent capital lockup on $14-15 pullback scenario)
Do not sell covered calls on stocks you intend to hold long-term; maintain separate core positions without call obligations
Accept assignment risk and don't buy back calls early unless forced to preserve long-term position, as buyback losses can be significant

strategy_candidates.change: Formalize position-sizing rule: allocate maximum 60% of weekly premium target to single highest-conviction stock (e.g., HIMS at $535), reserve 40% for diversified secondary positions (OLO, JOBY, new plays)
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth scaled HIMS to $15k (58% of capital deployed) while testing OLO, JOBY, Oscar smaller; achieved $1,192 premium on $25,930 capital but noted concentration risk—formalizing allocation would reduce single-stock earnings event impact

strategy_candidates.change: Screen and backtest entry timing rule: only initiate new covered-call positions after stock achieves 5+ day consolidation post-breakout, not at 52-week highs
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth explicitly avoided JOBY wheel strategy citing 10-day rip to all-time highs with pullback risk to $14-15; recognized Oscar Health had strong breakout but kept position small due to unfamiliarity—rule codifies this risk assessment into position entry

title: 7/21 part 8: $180 per week from HIMS covered calls?
